[[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称= 建布都神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿波市]]市場町香美字郷社本18 |祭神= [[建布都神]]
経津主…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 建布都神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿波市]]市場町香美字郷社本18 |祭神= [[建布都神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[事代主神]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿波市]]市場町香美に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。[[江戸時代]]までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。八幡神社との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、もう一つの論社は、阿波市[[土成町郡]]にある[[建布都西宮神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[建布都神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[事代主神]] == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * [[徳島自動車道]]「[[土成インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称= 建布都神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿波市]]市場町香美字郷社本18 |祭神= [[建布都神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[事代主神]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿波市]]市場町香美に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。[[江戸時代]]までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。[[八幡神社 (阿波市)|八幡神社]]との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、もう一つの論社は、阿波市[[土成町郡]]にある[[建布都西宮神社]]である。 == 祭神 == * [[建布都神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[事代主神]] == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * [[徳島自動車道]]「[[土成インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]]

{{wikidata link|Q48758365}}

{{神社 |名称= 建布都神社 |画像= [[File:阿波国式内社論社建布都神社(市場町香美).jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]字郷社本18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[建布都神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[事代主神]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。[[江戸時代]]までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。[[八幡神社 (阿波市)|八幡神社]]との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市[[土成町郡]]には[[建布都西宮神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * [[建布都神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[事代主神]] == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * [[徳島自動車道]]「[[土成インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]]

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

| native_name = 建布都神社

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Expand Japanese|建布都神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Takefutsu Shrine
| native_name = 建布都神社
| image = 阿波国式内社論社建布都神社(市場町香美).jpg
| mapframe = yes
| mapframe-zoom = 15
| mapframe-wikidata = yes

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Expand Japanese|建布都神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Takefutsu Shrine
| native_name = 建布都神社
| image = 阿波国式内社論社建布都神社(市場町香美).jpg
| mapframe = yes

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Expand Japanese|建布都神社}}

{{Infobox religious building
| name = Takefutsu Shrine
| native_name = 建布都神社
| image = 阿波国式内社論社建布都神社(市場町香美).jpg
| mapframe = yes

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

{{nihongo|'''Takefutsu Shrine'''|建布都神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]].

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 建布都神社 |画像= [[File:阿波国式内社論社建布都神社(市場町香美).jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]字郷社本18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[建布都神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[事代主神]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。[[江戸時代]]までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。[[八幡神社 (阿波市)|八幡神社]]との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市[[土成町郡]]には[[建布都西宮神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * [[建布都神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[事代主神]] == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * [[徳島自動車道]]「[[土成インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]]

[[経津主神]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[事代主神]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。[[江戸時代]]までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。[[八幡神社 (阿波市)|八幡神社]]との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市[[土成町郡]]には[[建布都西宮神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * [[建布都神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[事代主神]] == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * [[徳島自動車道]]「[[土成インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] ==End Japanese==

鳥居と拝殿 |所在地= [[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]字郷社本18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[建布都神]]
[[経津主神]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[事代主神]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿波市]][[市場町香美]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。[[江戸時代]]までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。[[八幡神社 (阿波市)|八幡神社]]との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市[[土成町郡]]には[[建布都西宮神社]]がある。 == 祭神 == * [[建布都神]] * [[経津主神]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[事代主神]] == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * [[徳島自動車道]]「[[土成インターチェンジ]]」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] ==End Japanese==

{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町香美|Q47393149}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 == 歴史 == [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。{{jalink|八幡神社 (阿波市)||八幡神社}}との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市{{jalink|土成町郡|Q28692234}}には{{jalink|建布都西宮神社|Q48758357}}がある。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|建布都神}} * {{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} * {{jalink|大山祇神}} * {{jalink|事代主神}} == 境内社 == * 天満宮 * 山神宮 == 交通 == * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 == 参考文献 == * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] ==End Japanese== [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]]

{{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}}
{{jalink|大山祇神}}
{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町香美|Q47393149}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。{{jalink|八幡神社 (阿波市)||八幡神社}}との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市{{jalink|土成町郡|Q28692234}}には{{jalink|建布都西宮神社|Q48758357}}がある。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建布都神}} * {{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} * {{jalink|大山祇神}} * {{jalink|事代主神}} === 境内社 === * 天満宮 * 山神宮 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{jalink|事代主神}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・{{jalink|旧郷社}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''建布都神社'''（たけふつじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|阿波市|Q791587}}{{jalink|市場町香美|Q47393149}}に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。 === 歴史 === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|建布都古墳]] 創建年は不詳。{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}までは平治権現またはまたは平地祠と称し、「おへーしさん」の愛称で知られた。{{jalink|八幡神社 (阿波市)||八幡神社}}との式内論争の末、明治に現社名に復称。境内には直径17m程度の円墳である建布都古墳が存在する。 『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に掲載されている建布都神社の論社の一つで、阿波市{{jalink|土成町郡|Q28692234}}には{{jalink|建布都西宮神社|Q48758357}}がある。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建布都神}} * {{jalink|経津主神|Q1107286}} * {{jalink|大山祇神}} * {{jalink|事代主神}} === 境内社 === * 天満宮 * 山神宮 === 交通 === * {{jalink|徳島自動車道|Q11490216}}「{{jalink|土成インターチェンジ|Q10927770}}」より車で約15分。 === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603） === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii and worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa City|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149}}, Aza Gōsha-moto 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Takefutsunushi}}
{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}}
{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi}}
{{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149}}, {{jalink|Awa City|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''. In {{jalink|Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{jalink|Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Takefutsunushi}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi}} * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{jalink|Donari Interchange|Q10927770}}" on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii and worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa City|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149}}, Aza Gōsha-moto 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Takefutsunushi}}
{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}}
{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi}}
{{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149}}, {{jalink|Awa City|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''. In {{jalink|Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{jalink|Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Takefutsunushi}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi}} * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{jalink|Donari Interchange|Q10927770}}" on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii and worship hall |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Awa City|Q791587}}{{jalink|Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149}}, Aza Gōsha-moto 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Takefutsunushi}}
{{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}}
{{jalink|Ōyamatsumi}}
{{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・{{jalink|former district shrine}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149}}, {{jalink|Awa City|Q791587}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{jalink|Hachiman Shrine}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}''. In {{jalink|Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{jalink|Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Takefutsunushi}} * {{jalink|Futsunushi|Q1107286}} * {{jalink|Ōyamatsumi}} * {{jalink|Kotoshironushi}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{jalink|Donari Interchange|Q10927770}}" on the {{jalink|Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill||lt=Hachiman Shrine|UNKNOWN}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill||lt=Takefutsunushi|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill||lt=Ōyamatsumi|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill||lt=Kotoshironushi|UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149}}, Aza Gōsha-moto 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149}}, {{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q11390939}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii and worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}{{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149}}, Aza Gōsha-moto 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149}}, {{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q11390939}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=UNKNOWN}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149|1=Ichibachō Kagami}}, {{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234|1=Donari-chō Kōri}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357|1=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234|1=Donari-chō Kōri}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357|1=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけふつしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]][[Category:qq]]

Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149|1=Ichibachō Kagami}}, {{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234|1=Donari-chō Kōri}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357|1=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]]

Torii and worship hall |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149|1=Ichibachō Kagami}}, Aza Gōsha-moto 18 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=former district shrine|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= |例祭= |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=建布都神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takefutsu Shrine''' (たけふつじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ichibachō Kagami|Q47393149|WD=Q47393149|1=Ichibachō Kagami}}, {{ill|lt=Awa City|Q791587|WD=Q791587|1=Awa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. === History === [[File:Takehutsu shrine (Tomb), Ichiba-cho, Tokushima.jpg|thumb|right|Takefutsu Tumulus]] The year of establishment is unknown. Until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was called Heiji Gongen or Heichi-shi, and was popularly known by the nickname "Ohēshi-san." After a dispute with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} over identification as the Shikinaisha, the shrine was restored to its current name in the Meiji era. Within the shrine grounds, there is the Takefutsu Tumulus, a circular tumulus approximately 17 meters in diameter. It is one of the candidate shrines (disputed identification) for Takefutsu Shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}''. In {{ill|lt=Donari-chō Kōri|Q28692234|WD=Q28692234|1=Donari-chō Kōri}}, Awa City, there is {{ill|lt=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine|Q48758357|WD=Q48758357|1=Takefutsu Nishinomiya Shrine}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takefutsunushi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === Subsidiary shrines === * Tenman-gū * Yamagami-gū === Access === * Approximately 15 minutes by car from "{{ill|lt=Donari Interchange|Q10927770|WD=Q10927770|1=Donari interchange}}" on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Expressway|Q11490216|WD=Q11490216|1=Tokushima Expressway}}. === References === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603) === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takefutsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:阿波市の歴史]] [[Category:阿波市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]]
